title: Day in the Life of an AI Engineer
transcript: I am a developer and I'm very good at Python and suddenly after 4.6 release my skills have no value. Everyone see AI as a magic but it is just a tool. So I need to now start how can I unlearn a few things? How can I remove the baggage I'm carrying as a developer? Welcome to Atli Technologies. Atli is an AI services company and we have a team of AI engineers here. Today I'm going to show you a day in the life of an AI engineer. I'm the co-founder of Atli and my involvement here is in sales, dealing with clients and [music] participating in the technical architecture for AI projects. I also do video shooting for my code basics work right from this office. The AI engineering team sits in the [music] next office so we are going to walk over there. Let's go. Okay. Hey Srikanth. Yeah, hi. Yeah, hi. How are you? Srikanth, you want to introduce yourself? Yeah, sure. My name is Srikanth Dongala and I'm working as an AI engineer at Atli Technologies. Okay. So we would like to know what kind of project you are working on and what tech stack do you use? So this is in the fintech domain. Okay. And what kind of technology [music] and tools do you use here? Most of the things back end is in Python and for the cloud we are using AWS. And we'll have a load balancer and lambda functions and we are using Fargate to manage all the load and concurrency handlers. So this is mostly and we also use Docker, we'll use Postman, you know. And in terms of LLM like which LLM do you use here? >> So we use OpenAI and also we are now recently testing the server models to test the capabilities of Indian models and see how Oh, wow. That's pretty amazing. Okay. [music] I want to know how does your day look like, typical day look like from morning till evening? These days [music] my day starts around 7:00 or 8:00 a.m. and I spend some time in researching, >> [music] >> collecting most important resources that I want to you know do research on or to look into for that particular day. Because every day new things are being released, right? Yeah, it is very difficult to you know catch up with everything but you'll have to try to do as much as you can. It's crazy. >> [laughter] >> So it's difficult to you know catch up everything but we'll have to try as much as we can. So I will start my day with you know identifying what all things that I need to cover for for that day and I will take short break for 1 or 1 and 1/2 hour and then I'll be back in office by around 9:30 or 10:00 ish and then the very first thing I do is check my emails and messages and I will complete that routine for about 5 or 10 minutes and then I will start planning my day, what all tasks that I need to do for that particular day. And once I do that planning I will pick my you know the most complex task for that particular day because in the morning we'll be fresh. Oh, that's a good idea. >> [laughter] >> So we I will you know start with the most challenging task for that particular day and you know I will be on it for my entire first half >> [music] >> and in parallel we'll also having some internal you know team sync ups and scrum calls. So that will be the priority for the first half. And I will try to keep most of the calls in the second half of the day because you know to make most of to you know better manage our time. So one thing that any beginner should remember is do not jump to a framework or a technique or a model directly. So understand the [music] problem and then pick the right tool that you actually need instead of jumping directly with GenAI or any ML.
